🇳🇱 Amsterdam Neighbourhood

Oud-West

One of Amsterdam's most lively and creative districts, Oud-West is where locals go for dinner and shopping when they want to avoid the tourist crowds of the centre.

Oud-West sits just outside the canal ring and has transformed from a quiet residential area into one of the city's most desirable neighbourhoods. Its heartbeat is the Ten Katemarkt and the adjacent De Hallen, a repurposed tram depot that now houses the city's best food hall, a cinema, and independent design shops.

The streets are lined with craft coffee shops, trendy boutiques, and some of the best international restaurants in the city. It's a neighbourhood that feels authentic, lived-in, and continuously evolving.

Things to Do

01

Foodhallen

A converted tram depot featuring over 20 different food stalls and several bars. The perfect spot for a social dinner.

02

Ten Katemarkt

A local, authentic street market that's much less touristy than the Albert Cuyp. Great for fresh produce and local snacks.

03

Jan-Pieter Heijestraat

One of the best streets for independent lifestyle shops and boutique shopping in the city.

Quick Facts

🍴
Character
Trendy, residential, foodie
📍
Location
West of the canal ring
🚲
Best way
Cycling
🌟
Best for
Local life, food halls

Getting There

🚋
Tram 7 or 17 to Ten Katestraat.
🚲
5-minute ride from the Jordaan.

the city?
